prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(open_incident_case primary_farm_unit_1)
(assign_supplier_to_incident primary_farm_unit_1 supplier_northern_agri)
(receive_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(confirm_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(retract_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(reserve_alternate_batch_for_primary_unit primary_farm_unit_1 route_option_1 batch_substitute_01)
(open_incident_case secondary_farm_unit_1)
(assign_supplier_to_incident secondary_farm_unit_1 supplier_southern_supplies)
(receive_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(confirm_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(retract_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(receive_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(finalize_primary_unit_preparation primary_farm_unit_1 route_option_1 delivery_estimate_1 batch_substitute_01)
(reserve_alternate_batch_for_secondary_unit secondary_farm_unit_1 transport_vehicle_truck_1 batch_substitute_01)
(retract_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(receive_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(finalize_secondary_unit_preparation secondary_farm_unit_1 transport_vehicle_truck_1 delivery_estimate_1 batch_substitute_01)
(reserve_shipment_primary_route_and_vehicle primary_farm_unit_1 secondary_farm_unit_1 route_option_1 transport_vehicle_truck_1 shipment_manifest_001)
(retract_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(receive_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(mark_shipment_loaded shipment_manifest_001 primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(final_repair_and_release_primary_unit primary_farm_unit_1 shipment_manifest_001)
(final_repair_and_release_secondary_unit secondary_farm_unit_1 shipment_manifest_001)
(assign_contingency_stock_to_incident primary_farm_unit_1 contingency_stock_item_01 delivery_estimate_1)
(apply_assigned_contingency_stock_to_primary_unit primary_farm_unit_1 supplier_northern_agri contingency_stock_item_01)
(retract_delivery_estimate primary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(receive_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(assign_contingency_stock_to_incident secondary_farm_unit_1 contingency_stock_item_01 delivery_estimate_1)
(apply_assigned_contingency_stock_to_secondary_unit secondary_farm_unit_1 supplier_southern_supplies contingency_stock_item_01)
(retract_delivery_estimate secondary_farm_unit_1 delivery_estimate_1)
(open_incident_case regional_coordinator_north)
(assign_supplier_to_incident regional_coordinator_north supplier_northern_agri)
(receive_delivery_estimate regional_coordinator_north delivery_estimate_1)
(confirm_delivery_estimate regional_coordinator_north delivery_estimate_1)
(collect_qc_sample_and_assign_to_shipment regional_coordinator_north qc_sample_batch_a shipment_manifest_001)
(coordinator_predeployment_check regional_coordinator_north qc_sample_batch_a shipment_manifest_001 delivery_estimate_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_incident regional_coordinator_north response_team_alpha)
(assign_transport_asset_to_coordinator regional_coordinator_north transport_asset_trailer_01)
(coordinator_authorization_stage1_assign_asset regional_coordinator_north transport_asset_trailer_01 response_team_alpha qc_sample_batch_a shipment_manifest_001)
(assign_specialist_to_coordinator regional_coordinator_north specialist_service_lab_01)
(coordinator_authorization_stage2_assign_specialist regional_coordinator_north specialist_service_lab_01 qc_sample_batch_a shipment_manifest_001)
(perform_salvage_deployment regional_coordinator_north)
(recall_response_team_from_incident regional_coordinator_north response_team_alpha)
(release_transport_asset_from_coordinator regional_coordinator_north transport_asset_trailer_01)
(release_specialist_from_coordinator regional_coordinator_north specialist_service_lab_01)
(assign_contingency_stock_to_incident regional_coordinator_north contingency_stock_item_01 delivery_estimate_1)
(apply_assigned_contingency_stock_by_coordinator regional_coordinator_north supplier_northern_agri contingency_stock_item_01)
(retract_delivery_estimate regional_coordinator_north delivery_estimate_1)
(open_incident_case regional_coordinator_south)
(assign_supplier_to_incident regional_coordinator_south supplier_northern_agri)
(receive_delivery_estimate regional_coordinator_south delivery_estimate_1)
(confirm_delivery_estimate regional_coordinator_south delivery_estimate_1)
(collect_qc_sample_and_assign_to_shipment regional_coordinator_south qc_sample_batch_b shipment_manifest_001)
(coordinator_predeployment_check regional_coordinator_south qc_sample_batch_b shipment_manifest_001 delivery_estimate_1)
(deploy_response_team_to_incident regional_coordinator_south response_team_alpha)
(assign_transport_asset_to_coordinator regional_coordinator_south transport_asset_trailer_01)
(coordinator_authorization_stage1_assign_asset regional_coordinator_south transport_asset_trailer_01 response_team_alpha qc_sample_batch_b shipment_manifest_001)
(assign_specialist_to_coordinator regional_coordinator_south specialist_service_lab_01)
(coordinator_authorization_stage2_assign_specialist regional_coordinator_south specialist_service_lab_01 qc_sample_batch_b shipment_manifest_001)
(perform_salvage_deployment regional_coordinator_south)
(assign_contingency_stock_to_incident regional_coordinator_south contingency_stock_item_01 delivery_estimate_1)
(apply_assigned_contingency_stock_by_coordinator regional_coordinator_south supplier_northern_agri contingency_stock_item_01)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a complex planning domain for fertilizer delivery delay contingency. The goal is to achieve recovery_complete for primary_farm_unit_1, secondary_farm_unit_1, regional_coordinator_north, and regional_coordinator_south.


## Examining problem scope  
- The task focuses on a complex planning domain for managing fertilizer delivery delays.  
- The goal is to achieve recovery_complete for multiple units: primary_farm_unit_1, secondary_farm_unit_1, regional_coordinator_north, and regional_coordinator_south.

## Examining problem scope  
- The task involves classical planning, suggesting sequential actions despite potential for parallel coordination.  
- Resources like suppliers and delivery estimates are limited, with only one delivery_estimate_1 available initially.  

## Identifying key actions  
- Recovery_complete for units and coordinators requires specific actions: apply_assigned_contingency_stock for primary/secondary units and coordinators.  
- These actions depend on incident_contingency_assigned and other preconditions like incident_ready_for_assignment.  

## Analyzing preconditions  
- Incident_ready_for_assignment can be set by actions like perform_salvage_deployment for regional_coordinators or final_repair_and_release for units.  
- Some actions require coordinator_deployment_completed, indicating multiple authorization stages in the process.  

## Exploring resource constraints  
- Shared resources, such as three suppliers, affect plan feasibility, with delivery estimates assigned per incident.  
- Links like unit_route_option_link are specific, such as only for primary_farm_unit_1 with route_option_1, limiting flexibility.

## Exploring shipment coordination  
- Reserve_shipment_primary_route_and_vehicle requires both primary and secondary sites to be ready and prepared, suggesting joint preparation.  
- Final_repair_and_release for both units needs shipment_reserved and shipment_loaded, indicating a single shipment process for both.  

## Analyzing unit preparation  
- Preparing secondary units involves selecting vehicle options and assigning delivery estimates, but only one delivery_estimate is available initially.  
- Assigning response teams to secondary units requires prior deployment, but only one team is available, necessitating a sequential approach.  

## Investigating backup options  
- Reserve_alternate_batch_for_primary_unit allows testing backup routes, setting primary_site_ready without requiring a response team.  
- Finalize_primary_unit_preparation then resets to primary options, making the batch available again after temporary reservation.

## Continuing unit preparation  
- Final_repair_and_release requires both primary and secondary units to be prepared, with shipment_loaded and shipment_reserved conditions met.  
- Mark_shipment_loaded needs the delivery estimate still assigned to the primary farm unit, ensuring continuity in the preparation process.  

## Examining delivery estimate management  
- Delivery estimates can be retracted from a unit after confirmation, making them available for reassignment to other units like secondary farm units.  
- Reassigning a delivery estimate is possible after retraction, as preconditions for receiving it don’t prohibit prior assignments, allowing flexibility in planning.  

## Sketching a unit coordination plan  
- The plan starts by opening an incident for the primary farm unit, assigning a supplier, and confirming the delivery estimate before retracting it for sharing.  
- Secondary units follow a similar process, reserving alternate batches sequentially to share resources like delivery estimates and batches, ensuring coordinated preparation.

## Continuing unit preparation  
- Recovery for each unit requires incident_ready status and assigning contingency stock with a delivery estimate.  
- For primary farm unit pfu1, contingency stock and supplier are applied, marking recovery_complete and freeing resources.  

## Managing secondary unit recovery  
- Secondary farm unit sfu1 needs the delivery estimate reassigned after retraction from pfu1 to proceed with contingency stock assignment.  
- Applying contingency stock to sfu1 with supplier_southern_supplies marks recovery_complete, making stock and supplier available again.  

## Coordinating recovery for regional units  
- Regional coordinators like north and south require incident_ready status to assign contingency stock, involving multiple agreement or authorization paths.  
- One path involves supplier agreements, response teams, and specialists to finalize and mark cases ready for deployment.

## Exploring coordinator paths  
- Assigning specialists to coordinators doesn’t require them to be unassigned initially, but once assigned, they’re no longer available for others until released.  
- Release_specialist_from_coordinator allows reassigning specialists after use, ensuring flexibility in resource allocation.  

## Managing response teams  
- Deploy_response_team_to_incident assigns teams for agreement confirmation, with recall_response_team_from_incident enabling reuse.  
- This mirrors specialist management, ensuring teams can be reassigned after initial use in operations.  

## Assessing agreement feasibility  
- The agreement path for south coordinator fails due to unavailability of supplier agreement partner2, linked to south but not accessible.  
- No actions exist to make partner2 available, limiting south coordinator to alternative paths.  

## Investigating salvage deployment  
- Perform_salvage_deployment sets incident_ready for coordinators without agreements, requiring prior deployment_authorized_by_coordinator.  
- This authorization comes from stage2 actions like coordinator_authorization_stage2_assign_specialist, involving specialists, QC, and shipments.

## Preparing coordinator for deployment  
- For regional coordinator north (rcn), stage1 requires no backup vehicle and uses coordinator_authorization_stage1_assign_asset, setting stage1 status.  
- Stage2 involves assigning specialist lab_01 to rcn after ensuring availability, then using coordinator_authorization_stage2_assign_specialist to authorize deployment.  

## Executing salvage deployment  
- Perform_salvage_deployment for rcn sets incident_ready, requiring prior deployment authorization and no completion flags.  
- After deployment, resources like teams, specialists, and assets can be released, but not immediately necessary for rcn.  

## Managing resource availability  
- For rc_south, resources like QC batch b and shipment remain available, potentially allowing multiple QC assignments to the same shipment.  
- Deploying to south requires recalling teams and releasing assets from north, ensuring no backup routes or vehicles are assigned.  

## Ensuring recovery readiness  
- To achieve incident_ready for rc_south, follow a similar sequence as rcn, including pre-deployment checks and salvage deployment.  
- Contingency stock assignment to rcn or rc_south depends on resource availability, with potential retraction and reassignment as needed.

## Detailing preparation sequence  
- Start with preparing primary farm unit 1 (pfu1) by opening incident case and assigning supplier northern_agri.  
- Include steps like receiving, confirming, and retracting delivery estimates, then reserving alternate batch for pfu1.  

## Preparing secondary unit  
- Prepare secondary farm unit 1 (sfu1) similarly, assigning supplier southern_supplies and handling delivery estimates.  
- Finalize pfu1 preparation first, then handle sfu1, ensuring both units are ready with selected routes and vehicles.  

## Finalizing unit readiness  
- Reserve shipment for both units using the selected route and vehicle, marking shipments loaded afterward.  
- Complete by finalizing repairs and releasing both units, achieving incident_ready status for recovery.  

## Managing recovery process  
- Assign contingency stock to pfu1 and apply it, ensuring stock availability for recovery.  
- Recovery for units releases suppliers, making northern and southern suppliers available again for reassignment.

## Continuing preparation sequence  
- Assign supplier northern_agri to primary farm unit 1, followed by receiving and confirming delivery estimate 1.  
- Retract delivery estimate 1 for primary farm unit 1 after confirmation, then reserve alternate batch with route option 1 and batch substitute 01.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
